name: Apricot-Pineapple Pie (Corky)
recipeInstructions:
1. This filling is intense and sweet-tart, like the traditional apricot-pineapple jam.
2. With a knife or kitchen shears, cut each apricot into quarters. Put the apricots in a saucepan, add water, bring to boil, cover and cook over medium heat for 10 minutes. Add sugar and cook for an additional 5 minutes. Drain, reserving 3/4 cup juice. Set the apricots aside.
3. Drain the crushed pineapple, reserving 1/4 cup juice. Set the pineapple aside.
4. In a mixing bowl, dissolve cornstarch in the reserved pineapple juice. Add the reserved apricot juice. Put the mixed juices in a saucepan, add a dash of salt and cook over medium heat until the mixture thickens, stirring continually.
5. Mix the drained apricots and pineapple thoroughly. Mix with the thickened juices and pour into an unbaked pie shell. Dot with butter. Cover with the top crust, crimp the edges and pierce with a fork. Bake at 400°F for 25 minutes. Makes 6-8 servings.
- Each serving contains about: 546 calories; 299 milligrams sodium; 16 milligrams cholesterol; 29 grams fat; 70 grams carbohydrates; 5 grams protein; 1.21 grams fiber.
